title: Intense Italian Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A salty, dense Neapolitan-inspired carbonara infused with cocoa and tempered chocolate for a decadent date-night treat.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil and cook spaghetti until al dente, about 9 minutes.
While the pasta cooks, dice pancetta into small cubes and heat a drizzle of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add pancetta to the skillet and cook until it renders its fat and turns golden crisp, about 5 minutes.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, pecorino, parmigiano, cocoa powder, and a generous grind of fresh black pepper until smooth.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving a cup of pasta water, and immediately toss it in the skillet with the pancetta, letting the hot pasta soak up the rendered fat.

Remove the skillet from heat and pour the egg mixture over the pasta, stirring rapidly to create a silky sauce without scrambling the eggs; add reserved pasta water a tablespoon at a time to adjust consistency.
While the sauce thickens, melt the dark chocolate over low heat and temper it by stirring in the cocoa powder until glossy and smooth.
Drizzle the tempered chocolate mixture over the carbonara, folding gently to incorporate a subtle chocolate undertone.
Plate the dish, garnish with a pinch of extra pecorino, a few cracked peppercorns, and a sprig of fresh parsley if desired.
Serve immediately, ensuring a warm, dense plate perfect for a romantic evening.
